100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On May 12, 1926, tensions escalated in the United Kingdom as a general strike by trade unions began, affecting various industries and prompting widespread disruption. In the United States, Congress continued to debate the future of Prohibition, with public sentiment shifting increasingly towards reform. Meanwhile, in Germany, political instability intensified as the Weimar Republic faced ongoing challenges from extremist factions.
